description: Effect of WRF damping (w_damping=1 + damp_opt=3 Rayleigh + smdiv/emdiv divergence) on the Gen2 d02 replay 12-step transaction audit at the operational dt. Compares the bare acoustic core (Sprint A, dampers off) to the F7-B damped core.

wrf_damping_coefficients.source: Gen2 surface_geo_v2_1/physics_sweep/_baseline/namelist.input + WRF Registry defaults; share/module_model_constants.F:88-89 for w_alpha/w_beta

runs.label: dampers_off_sprintA
runs.note: Sprint A baseline: acoustic/gravity ringing detonates by step 4 (u/v ~5e5, w ~1.5e4 m/s).

runs.label: wrf_damping_on_f7b
runs.first_critical_invariant: theta_sanity_bounds (min theta=198.9 K vs 200 K audit floor)
runs.note: WRF damping keeps the first ~5 steps physical (w~17 m/s, NOT 1e4); a residual growing mode escapes near step 6-7 and detonates by step 8. The remaining stabiliser gap is the WRF 6th-order numerical filter (diff_6th_opt=2), implemented and wired via --diff-6th-opt; see audit_operational_dt_diff6/.

verdict: AC3 PARTIAL: damping makes the operational-dt transients physical (O(10 m/s)) for the first ~5 steps vs Sprint A's immediate detonation, but the 12-step audit is not fully clean (first_critical not null). No masking clamp used; the residual growth needs the 6th-order/Smagorinsky operational filter set.
